purpose: Tracks architecture reference exhibits, prototype files, adopter files, candidate status, and back-propagation obligations so migrations stay aligned with the latest pattern.

patterns.name: Feature screen route boundary

adopters.role: reference route screen: provider watch, async/error branches, loaded Scaffold, bottom navigation, route callbacks, mutation listeners, EventDetailScreenState host/companion/booking derivation, and route side effects

provider-free EventDetailHostState, EventDetailCompanionState, and EventDetailBookingDockState adapters for club lookup display data, host message availability, companion plan availability, booking dock modes, mutation display flags, and consumer-action visibility
provider-free Event Detail display-state definitions for companion availability, host card data, host stats, section visibility, invite-loop visibility, and social/review access
reference controller seam for event-detail side effects and save mutation

embedded scroll body for reference route path; requires explicit save/share/calendar/back callbacks, companion state, host state, route-intent callbacks, and has no direct Scaffold, bottom navigation, provider reads, mutation listeners, or route side effects
provider-free host, companion, and booking dock display-state behavior coverage
canonical feature-screen inventory and screen composition map
route-state and body-state review coverage for the reference screen
adopter route screen: EventDetailViewModel watch, chromeless route Scaffold, coordinate gate, provider-free map body, and external directions callback owned by the route
provider-free display-state adapter for exact coordinate availability, map tile mode, location copy, and directions URI
focused route/body/state tests for Event Location Map screen-boundary adoption
screen-capture entries mount EventLocationMapRouteScreen for route chrome states instead of body-owned scaffold compatibility
Widgetbook route and body states use route provider overrides or explicit EventLocationMapState/callback fixtures
adopter route screen: uid/event provider waves, CalendarHomeState construction, route Scaffold, sliver scroll state, retry invalidation, and event-detail navigation callbacks
provider-free CalendarHomeState and CalendarEventSummary adapters for selected date, header mode, event merge/sort, and club-id lookup input
route shell, async/error, scroll, date selection, summary, and event-detail navigation coverage for Calendar
CalendarHomeState and CalendarEventSummary behavior coverage

adopter route screen: uid/saved-event provider wave, route Scaffold, async/error/empty states, retry invalidation, SavedEventsListState construction, club-name lookup, and saved-event detail navigation
provider-free SavedEventsListState adapter for event ordering, saved/past badge labels, tile statuses, today, and club-id lookup input
SavedEventsListState plus Saved Events route empty, ordering, and navigation coverage

adopter route screen: route-owned Explore feed/clubs/city/search/filter/source provider wave, body-state branch precedence, map launcher, mutation listener, empty-state callbacks, event/external navigation side effects, and provider-free chrome/body composition
adopter route screen: route-owned Explore map feed/filter provider wave, map view-model construction, retry invalidation, distance-filter cycling, and event-detail navigation side effects
provider-free Explore body/sliver composer for feed async states, partial club-section failure, optional legacy club rails, activity browse callbacks, and event/external row callbacks

provider-free Explore events section shell for feed loading/error/data branch selection, empty clear/broaden callbacks, mixed-card sliver assembly, and route-owned event/external/club selection callbacks
provider-free Explore event row widgets and this-week recommendation section receiving explicit row state and route-owned event/external callbacks
provider-free Explore club card and compact club row widgets receiving explicit club data and route-owned club-selection callback
provider-free Explore event loading and empty slivers receiving explicit empty-state actions and retry/clear callbacks
provider-free Explore filter rail and sheet receiving explicit filter state, sheet state, and route-owned filter mutation callbacks
provider-free Explore browse header and discovery cover header receiving explicit search text, featured item, and route-owned search/event callbacks
Explore route/body/chrome provider-boundary coverage including provider-free body, events, filter, header fixtures plus route-level provider override tests

defined host workspace variant: canonical route aliases, uid/club/event provider wave, access gate, loaded Setup/Guests/Live/Report workspace, roster/private-access/Event Success/report/action section composition

variantPatterns.name: Host workspace route boundary
variantPatterns.exitCriteria: Promote Host Event Manage from defined variant to aligned adopter only when the loaded workspace is provider-free for selected section, roster, private access, invite links, Event Success, report, host action display data, mutation display state, retry intents, navigation, and typed callbacks while HostEventManageRouteScreen keeps canonical route aliases plus route loading, missing-resource, error, and access gates. As of 2026-07-03 HostEventManageScreenState is provider-free, but HostEventManageScreen still owns loaded-workspace provider watches and HostEventAttendancePanel still has WidgetRef-parameter debt, so the variant remains open.

variantPatterns.name: Explore provider-island compatibility variants
variantPatterns.exitCriteria: Delete ExploreList if it remains only a legacy fixture/compatibility sliver, or migrate it to the route-owned ExploreBody contract. Promote ExploreCityPicker only when the route/chrome owns city-list/profile/location listeners and passes display state plus typed city callbacks. Promote ExplorePeekRail only when the map sheet lead is either deleted as non-production compatibility code or receives feed async state, retry, analytics, and event navigation callbacks from a route/screen boundary.

variantPatterns.name: Event Success staged screen boundary
variantPatterns.exitCriteria: Promote Event Success only after companion and host route screens are thin provider edges, display-state derivation lives in registered *_screen_state.dart adapters, provider-free part widgets remain render-only, and tests cover the host and companion screen-state adapters. The companion screen-state adapter, host screen-state adapter, and live reveal action-state adapter are aligned as of 2026-07-03; route-level ref density and route splitting remain open.

candidateQueue.reason: ChatsList still re-watches route providers for celebration and display-state construction. Keep this as explicit screen-boundary migration debt; do not mark the Chats inbox route aligned until the route owns those reads and passes display state/callbacks down.

Event Success companion now has a registered provider-free screen-state adapter, but the route screen still contains the provider wave, multiple route/render classes, and callback construction. Keep as screen-boundary debt until the route is a thin provider edge and the render surface receives explicit state/callbacks.

Event Success host now has provider-free host section/action state, but the route still owns a broad provider wave, mutation callback construction, and multiple route/render surfaces. Split the route/workspace boundary before marking the host surface aligned.

Host Operations is a 5k+ line multi-route file with multiple Consumer screens and a broad provider/ref surface. HostClubsScreenState and HostClubInsightsState are now extracted, but the file still needs route/workspace splits before applying screen-boundary adoption claims.

HostEventAttendancePanel remains a large widget with WidgetRef parameter debt and should receive route-owned attendance display state, mutation intents, and callbacks before the Host Event Manage workspace can be promoted.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When this exhibit changes, inspect each aligned adopter and every variant path above; update EventDetailScreen/EventDetailBody first, then EventLocationMapRouteScreen/EventLocationMapState/EventLocationMapScreen, CalendarScreen/CalendarHomeState, SavedEventsScreen/SavedEventsListState, and HostEventManageRouteScreen/ARCH-SCREEN-001C before migrating any later screen from this pattern.
patterns.notes: This pattern defines the feature-screen boundary. EventDetailBody and EventLocationMapScreen no longer expose direct Scaffold compatibility; route chrome, bottom navigation, mutation listeners, provider reads, save/share/calendar/back side effects, companion/host/booking display state, and navigation side effects belong to their route screens. EventDetailScreen now delegates host, companion, and booking dock display-state derivation to EventDetailScreenState while EventDetailCta remains the mutation/router edge for booking actions. EventLocationMapRouteScreen, CalendarScreen, SavedEventsScreen, ExploreScreen, and ExploreMapScreen are aligned adopters outside Event Detail and use provider-free state adapters or provider-free body/chrome widgets for their loaded body data. tool/architecture/check_adopted_architecture_boundaries.mjs enforces provider/routing/data/repository-free imports for aligned lib adopters with providerFree set to true. HostEventManageRouteScreen is intentionally tracked as ARCH-SCREEN-001C because its loaded surface is a host workspace with canonical route aliases and lifecycle sections; do not force it through the Event Detail body shape before the host workspace adapter exists. ExploreList, ExploreCityPicker, and ExplorePeekRail remain explicitly tracked under ARCH-SCREEN-001D until they are deleted or migrated to route-owned state/callback seams.

patterns.name: Provider-free presentation state model

adopters.role: route-edge provider reads and CalendarHomeState construction
reference CalendarHomeState and CalendarEventSummary implementation

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When this exhibit changes, inspect every adopter above and either update it in the same pass or mark it variant_needed/exception with a stable debt id.
requiredChecks: flutter test test/profile/profile_widgets_test.dart --plain-name 'text edit failure keeps the inline editor open with an error'
patterns.notes: This is a narrow display-state reference. CalendarHomeState remains the reference. CatchAsyncState is the provider-free async branch primitive for route-edge translations. EventDetailHostState, EventDetailCompanionState, and EventDetailBookingDockState are aligned small adapters for host-card, companion entry, and booking dock display data; EventDetailCta keeps provider mutations and router side effects at the edge. CatchesEventScreenState is aligned for queue provider-wave mapping, empty/access copy, and active deck display data; SwipeScreen now owns CatchesProfileReviewActionState for write-pending/failure display while the remaining Catches Event work is capture/reference coverage. HostClubDetailScreenState and ClubDetailBodyState are aligned for Club Detail route async branch mapping, live data, initial route fallback, host public-preview mode, next-event selection, contact actions, host-message eligibility, event-route target, review visibility, membership dock state, and retry typing; capture work remains open under the Club Detail screen contract. ExploreFeedSectionState, ExploreCoverStoryState, ExploreChromeState, ExploreEventRowState, ExploreExternalEventRowState, ExploreClubCardState, ExploreMapLauncherState, ExploreCityTriggerState, ExploreFilterRailState, ExploreFilterSheetState, ExploreCollapsedMapSummaryState, ExplorePeekRailState, ExploreMapEventTicketState, ExploreScreenBodyState, ExploreDiscoveryEmptyState, and ExploreEventsEmptyState are aligned adopters for mixed-feed planning, cover-story/search-open/event-row/external-row/club-card/map-sheet/filter/chrome display data, route/body branch, map launcher, and empty-state actions. EventSuccessRevealActionState is aligned as a provider-free live reveal action-state adapter after the host route now translates mutation state into plain pending/error snapshots. SelfProfileScreenState and SelfProfileEditTabState are aligned provider-free Profile adopters after the route provider split and photo intent-factory rename. ChatsListScreenState, HostChatScreenState, ChatThreadLookupState, ChatReadMarkerState, and ChatRouteState are aligned provider-free Chats adopters after the route view-model split and batched inbox roster lookups. The main Explore route/feed body migration is tracked under ARCH-SCREEN-001; only the explicitly recorded ARCH-SCREEN-001D provider islands remain. The Chats inbox widget-level route reads are recorded as ARCH-SCREEN-001-CHATS-INBOX-BOUNDARY candidate debt, not as aligned screen-boundary adoption.
